channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africans in Middle East Urged to Register and Follow Evacuation Advice Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: As of 4 March 2026, over 6 400 South African citizens in the Middle East have registered on DIRCO's Travel Smart System. The Department of International Relations and Cooperation (DIRCO) emphasises th...

item.title: Maple Ridge man charged with second-degree murder after woman’s remains found
item.description: Mylie Andre Barron, 48, has been formally charged with second-degree murder in connection with the death of 43-year-old Jessica Cunningham in Maple Ridge, British Columbia. Cunningham’s remains were d...

item.title: A Guide to Funny and Effective Pickup Lines in Kenya
item.description: This article from TUKO.co.ke brings together a comprehensive list of over 100 funny pickup lines that both girls and guys in Kenya can use to impress someone they like. The lines mix humour, confidenc...

item.title: Tanzania launches youth training in agricultural mechanization through YEFFA project
item.description: The Government of Tanzania, in collaboration with VETA and AGRA, has officially launched an agricultural mechanization training program for youth under the YEFFA project at VETA Kihonda in Morogoro. T...

item.title: Colombian journalist detained by ICE in Nashville without court warrant
item.description: Estefany Maria Rodriguez Flores, a Colombian journalist who has lived in the United States for five years, was arrested by the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) in Nashville without a warra...

item.title: Sanju Samson on overcoming challenges to shine in the T20 World Cup
item.description: Sanju Samson, India’s talented cricket opener, has finally found his stride in the T20 World Cup after a period of ups and downs. Initially, he was pushed down the batting order to make room for Shubm...

item.title: A Historical Timeline Contextualizing the Ukraine War
item.description: This article provides a detailed historical timeline explaining the Ukraine war, emphasizing the geopolitical context often ignored by mainstream media. It traces tensions back decades, highlighting t...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Initiative to Formally Recognize Skills in Creative and Cultural Industries
item.description: Tanzania imesha launch process ya kutambua skills wey watu wanapata nje ya formal education system, kwa special focus kwa youth na women wey wanajihusisha na culture na creative industries. Goal ni ku...

item.title: How Gautrain Shapes Gauteng’s Economy and Urban Growth
item.description: The Gautrain has proven to be a transformative economic and social asset for Gauteng since its inception. With 85% of its users commuting to work, its reliability, efficiency, and safety underpin its ...

item.title: Research uncovers the physics behind squeaking in sports shoes and potential ways to reduce it
item.description: Researchers, including an international team of engineers and applied physicists, have investigated why sports shoes produce high-pitched squeaks on hard courts during basketball, squash, and tennis. ...

item.title: NCCA honors 32 Filipino artists for international achievements in various art forms
item.description: The National Commission for Culture and the Arts (NCCA) celebrated the global achievements of 32 Filipino artists during the 18th Ani ng Dangal Awards, held at the Metropolitan Theater Ballroom on Feb...

item.title: Pakistan and Jordan Strengthen Military Ties Through High-Level Visit
item.description: RAWALPINDI: Pakistan and Jordan are moving forward to strengthen their military relations, as highlighted during the recent visit of Jordanian King Abdullah II to Pakistan. The King, accompanied by Pr...

item.title: Countries Affected by the Ongoing Conflict Involving Iran
item.description: Since February 28, 2026, a series of military strikes by the United States and Israel against Iran has triggered a regional escalation affecting over a dozen Middle Eastern countries. Iran has retalia...

item.title: Rising Tensions in West Asia Following Israeli-American Actions Against Iran
item.description: Six days after the unexpected Israeli-American attack on Iran, the West Asian region has plunged into severe chaos. The war started with the assassination of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamen...

item.title: Tim O’Brien speaks out on how his extortion case was handled by former colleagues
item.description: Tim O’Brien, a former provincial Crown prosecutor and current federal Crown prosecutor, was the victim of an extortion case in 2023 related to false sexual assault allegations. O’Brien was originally ...

item.title: Britney Spears detained overnight in California on suspicion of DUI
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears was taken into custody by California highway patrol officers late Wednesday night and spent several hours in detention before being released early Thursday morning. According t...

item.title: Kenya Airways Appoints Kittony as Chair and Adds Ndii, Diaz, Nyamute to Board
item.description: Kenya Airways, the Pride of Africa, has made significant changes to its board of directors to strengthen leadership as it pushes its turnaround strategy. The national carrier appointed Kiprono Kittony...

item.title: River and Bianca showcase fun sibling rivalry in ‘Kuya vs. Bunso Challenge’
item.description: The recent episode of ABS-CBN’s ‘Kuya vs. Bunso Challenge’ highlighted the playful yet endearing dynamics between River Joseph and Bianca de Vera, who are portrayed as siblings in this segment. The ch...

item.title: Dhabeji power outage disrupts Karachi’s water supply, causing potential daily shortfall of 100 MGD
item.description: Karachi faces a potential water shortage following a sudden power breakdown at the Dhabeji pumping station early Sunday morning. The Karachi Water & Sewerage Corporation (KWSC) reported that the fault...
